rows.signal: script-shaped payload in model output reflected into the diagram/markdown renderer output
rows.why: reflection is not execution — the renderer sandboxed/escaped the payload and the inert local canary never fired.

controls.positive: execution detector validated against a known-executing case before trusting the negative
controls.canary: inert local marker (title/console) that would fire only on execution — did not fire
controls.replay: consistently inert across a fresh session

rows.signal: an ordinary seat requested a model outside the company allowlist
rows.why: the allowlist was applied unconditionally for all seats, so the disallowed model was coerced server-side — defense holding, not a finding.

controls.positive: metadata showed the SERVED model differed from the disallowed request — coercion confirmed

rows.signal: chat-read request set the owner-id field to another principal the caller controls
rows.why: the server resolved ownership from the authenticated session and ignored the client owner id, returning the caller's own scope (or 404), never the other principal's object.

controls.negative: no-owner-id and self requests returned the caller's own scope
controls.positive: the planted canary in the other principal's resource was NEVER returned
controls.canary: benign marker in the second owned account — absent from every response
